domain_context:
This is a retail and e-commerce business dataset.
Revenue is the net sales value after discounts. Cost is the cost of goods sold.
Target revenue is the planned monthly sales budget.
MTD = month-to-date (cumulative from the 1st of the month to today).
YTD = year-to-date (cumulative from Jan 1 to today).
QTD = quarter-to-date (cumulative from quarter start to today).
Prev period refers to the same period in the previous year.
A sales rep or product is underperforming when MTD revenue is below both the monthly target and the previous year's MTD.
AOV (average order value) = total revenue / number of orders.
Run rate = projected month-end revenue at the current daily pace.

having_patterns.description: Filter rows above or below the average value of a metric
Rank rows by distance from target, closest first
having_patterns.sql_template: HAVING ABS(SUM({tgt_col}) - {agg}({col})) > 0 ORDER BY ABS(SUM({tgt_col}) - {agg}({col})) ASC

having_patterns.description: Filter rows where one metric is above or below another

rules.name: both_negative_required_columns
rules.description: When question asks for both-ve / underperforming, always include revenue, target_revenue and prev_revenue so comparison is visible.

rules.name: both_positive_required_columns
rules.description: When question asks for both+ve / overperforming, include revenue, target_revenue and prev_revenue for comparison.

rules.name: highlight_adds_achievement_pct
rules.description: When user says highlight or refers to underperforming/overperforming, automatically add target achievement % so the gap is quantified.

rules.name: mtd_comparison_requires_both_mtd_columns
rules.description: When MTD vs target or MTD vs last year is requested, ensure both mtd_revenue and the comparison metric are always in SELECT.

rules.name: cumulative_comparison_adds_growth_pct
rules.description: When cumulative metrics (MTD/YTD/QTD) are used, automatically add the corresponding growth % formula for context.

rules.name: average_metric_adds_volume_context
rules.description: When an average metric (e.g. satisfaction, delivery days) is queried, also include orders_count so the average is contextualised by volume.

rules.name: gap_to_target_inline_formula
rules.description: When user asks about gap or shortfall to target, create an inline formula showing the absolute gap value.

rules.name: variance_vs_prev_inline_formula
rules.description: When user asks about variance or change vs previous period, create an inline formula for the absolute variance.

rules.name: mtd_gap_inline_formula
rules.description: When user asks specifically about MTD gap or MTD shortfall, create an MTD-specific gap formula using cumulative columns.

phrases: how much to target this month

rules.name: achievement_pct_uses_ytd_in_ytd_context
rules.description: When question is about YTD context, override target_achievement_pct to use ytd_revenue and ytd_target.

rules.name: run_rate_uses_qtd_in_quarterly_context
rules.description: When run rate is requested in a quarterly context, override revenue_run_rate to use qtd_revenue.

rules.name: tabular_format_hint
rules.description: When user asks for tabular format, set output to programmatic enumeration table.

rules.name: comparison_table_for_two_periods
rules.description: When question compares two periods, render as a side-by-side comparison table.

rules.name: lowest_performing_sort_asc
rules.description: Surface worst performers first when question asks for lowest/worst.

rules.name: top_performing_sort_desc
rules.description: Surface best performers first when question asks for top/highest/best.

rules.name: satisfaction_sort_asc_for_lowest
rules.description: When asking for lowest satisfaction, sort ascending by satisfaction score.

rules.name: delivery_sort_desc_for_slowest
rules.description: When asking for slowest delivery, sort descending by delivery_days.
